story_assessments.story_title: OpenAI’s new scorecard points AI buyers toward workflow economics
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, small-business owners, AI buyers, and ops teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want proof that AI saves time, reduces cleanup, and improves finished output rather than just producing impressive drafts.
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said AI adoption deepens in stages, and argued companies should measure useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and whether each AI dollar buys more work at scale.
story_assessments.stop_reason: It replaces vague hype with a practical scorecard for real workflows.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A simple way to test whether one AI workflow is actually worth keeping.
story_assessments.proof_point: Confirmed from OpenAI’s published scorecard framing and metrics.
story_assessments.score_reason: Broad audience, immediate business value, and a very clear testable takeaway make this the strongest utility story in the pack.

story_assessments.story_title: GPT-Red suggests safety is becoming a production system, not a side project
story_assessments.target_audience: Teams using connected AI tools, security-minded users, and product builders
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want AI that can use tools without being tricked into unsafe actions or exposing private data.
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said it trained GPT-Red, an automated internal red-teaming model, and used it to adversarially train GPT-5.6 against prompt injection.
story_assessments.stop_reason: Safety is being folded into training, not bolted on afterward.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A clearer rule for keeping human approval on sensitive AI actions.
story_assessments.proof_point: Confirmed automated internal red-teaming and GPT-5.6 hardening were described by OpenAI.
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong practical consequence and a crisp safety takeaway, though the topic is slightly more technical and niche than the workflow-economics story.

story_assessments.story_title: Google’s ad disclosure move hints that AI labels are becoming standard
story_assessments.target_audience: Advertisers, creators, small brands, and marketing teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need to know when AI-edited creative requires disclosure and how to document assets cleanly.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google said it is adding a "How this ad was made" panel and requiring labels for ads created or edited with generative AI.
story_assessments.stop_reason: Disclosure is becoming built into the ad product itself.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A simple rule for keeping prompts, edit history, and disclosure text with each asset.
story_assessments.proof_point: Confirmed Google transparency changes on Search, YouTube, and Discover.
search_phrases: How this ad was made panel
story_assessments.score_reason: This is highly searchable, easy to explain, and immediately relevant to marketers and creators who handle ad assets.

story_assessments.story_title: ASML’s higher forecast says the AI buildout is still running hot
story_assessments.target_audience: AI investors, infrastructure watchers, and tech ops teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to know whether AI infrastructure pressure is easing or still expanding.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Reuters reported that ASML raised its 2026 sales forecast and plans to expand capacity by 30% in each of the next two years because of AI chip and data-center demand.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The hardware layer is still scaling, which keeps compute economics in focus.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A practical reason to budget AI as recurring operating spend.
story_assessments.proof_point: Confirmed Reuters reporting via Investing.com on the forecast and capacity expansion.
search_phrases: ASML AI chip demand capacity expansion
story_assessments.score_reason: Useful as a macro signal, but less universally actionable than the workflow, safety, disclosure, or family-control stories.

story_assessments.story_title: OpenAI’s teen-safety push shows consumer AI is moving toward household controls
story_assessments.target_audience: Families, education product teams, consumer AI users, and youth-safety watchers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want age-appropriate access, parental controls, and clearer safeguards around consumer AI.
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said it strengthened teen protections, rolled out age prediction, and expanded parental controls.
story_assessments.stop_reason: Consumer AI is moving toward household governance.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A clearer view of where family controls may become the standard expectation.
story_assessments.proof_point: Confirmed teen-safety rollout and continued safeguard additions were described by OpenAI.
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong consumer trust angle with clear household relevance, though slightly narrower than the workflow-economics story.

tiktok.story_title: OpenAI’s new scorecard points AI buyers toward workflow economics
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, small-business owners, AI buyers, and ops teams
tiktok.opening: Is your AI saving time or just making drafts?
tiktok.payoff: Use OpenAI’s scorecard idea to test one workflow by measuring completed work, cost per task, and cleanup.
tiktok.selection_reason: Short-form audiences respond well to a concrete self-test, and this story gives them an easy, practical challenge.

youtube.story_title: GPT-Red suggests safety is becoming a production system, not a side project
youtube.target_audience: Teams using connected AI tools, security-minded users, and product builders
youtube.opening: Can your AI read files safely?
youtube.payoff: OpenAI said GPT-Red was used to harden GPT-5.6 against prompt injection, so connected tools need a human checkpoint for sensitive actions.
youtube.selection_reason: The safety angle is easy to search, easy to explain quickly, and has a clear operational takeaway for connected AI workflows.

facebook.story_title: Google’s ad disclosure move hints that AI labels are becoming standard
facebook.target_audience: Advertisers, creators, small brands, and marketing teams
facebook.opening: Do your ads need an AI label now?
facebook.payoff: Google is adding a "How this ad was made" panel and requiring labels for generative-AI-made or edited ads, so teams should keep prompts, edit history, and disclosure text together.
facebook.selection_reason: This is a recognizable business situation with a concrete documentation consequence, which fits Facebook’s discussion style well.

pinterest.story_title: OpenAI’s new scorecard points AI buyers toward workflow economics
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, small-business owners, AI buyers, and ops teams
pinterest.opening: How do you know if AI actually saves time?
pinterest.payoff: Track one workflow end to end and compare time, error rate, and human cleanup before deciding whether to keep the tool.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest rewards evergreen, searchable how-to guidance, and this story naturally becomes a checklist people can save.

linkedin.story_title: OpenAI’s new scorecard points AI buyers toward workflow economics
linkedin.target_audience: Creators, small-business owners, AI buyers, and ops teams
linkedin.opening: Are you measuring AI output or AI usage?
linkedin.payoff: OpenAI’s framing points teams toward useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and less human rework.
linkedin.selection_reason: Workflow metrics and operational consequences map directly to LinkedIn’s professional audience and team decision-making.

overall_reasoning: Story 0 is the strongest general utility angle, so it anchors TikTok, Pinterest, and LinkedIn with a measurable workflow payoff. Story 1 is the best short-form safety hook for YouTube Shorts because prompt-injection risk is easy to explain quickly. Story 2 is the best Facebook fit because ad disclosure is a recognizable business decision with a concrete documentation takeaway. The quote lines stay calm, practical, and discussion-ready while staying inside the verified research pack.
